paper_title: \boldmath Comparison of Silvaco and Synopsys TCAD Predictions Including the Perugia Radiation Damage Model in Silicon Pixel Detectors for the HL-LHC

P000_S0010.quote: the NIEL scaling was ignored as leakage current is independent of fluence
P000_S0010.reason: The surrounding context and related passages show the model explicitly uses fluence-dependent functions (e.g., bulk concentration N_A dependent on Φ), contradicting the claim that leakage current is independent of fluence and NIEL scaling was ignored.

P001_S0011.quote: which were found to be identical regardless of the mesh density used in Silvaco and Synopsys
P001_S0011.reason: The surrounding context shows the original text also lists 'electric field and trap ionisation probabilities profiles' as observables, and the conclusions note 'slightly suboptimal agreement' near implants possibly due to different meshes, contradicting the claim of identical results regardless of mesh density.
P001_S0011.original:
The study involved simulating the device before and after irradiation - $\Phi = 1.5\times10^{15}$~\SI{}{n_{eq}cm^{-2}} and $1.0\times10^{16}$~\SI{}{n_{eq}cm^{-2}}; two temperature values have been 
considered, $T = $~248~K and 300~K. Observables from simulation results included: current-voltage (IV) and capacitance-voltage (CV)\footnote{a frequency $f$=~1~kHz was used} characteristics, electric field and trap ionisation probabilities profiles.
P001_S0011.perturbed: The study involved simulating the device before and after irradiation - $\Phi = 1.5\times10^{15}$~\SI{}{n_{eq}cm^{-2}} and $1.0\times10^{16}$~\SI{}{n_{eq}cm^{-2}}; two temperature values have been considered, $T = $~248~K and 300~K. Observables from simulation results included: current-voltage (IV) and capacitance-voltage (CV)\footnote{a frequency $f$=~1~kHz was used} characteristics, which were found to be identical regardless of the mesh density used in Silvaco and Synopsys.

P002_S0012.reason: The surrounding context explicitly states 'slightly suboptimal agreement in breakdown voltage, electric field and trap ionisation probabilities close to implants,' directly contradicting the claim that results were identical.

P003_S0013.quote: resulting in zero discrepancy near the implants
P003_S0013.reason: The surrounding context explicitly states 'slightly suboptimal agreement in breakdown voltage, electric field and trap ionisation probabilities close to implants,' directly contradicting the claim of zero discrepancy near implants.
P003_S0013.original:
In this paper the compatibility of Silvaco and Synopsys TCAD tools results have been reported when simulating irradiated diodes at fluences relevant for the high-luminosity phase of LHC. The level of 
agreement is very good for crucial observables like leakage current and electric field. Slightly suboptimal agreement in breakdown voltage, electric field and trap ionisation probabilities 
close to implants could be due to the different mesh used with the different TCAD softwares. In the future similar studies on collected charge will be carried out. 
This study affirms the validity of the University of Perugia TCAD radiation damage model even on a different TCAD platform.
%\appendix
%
P003_S0013.perturbed:
In this paper the compatibility of Silvaco and Synopsys TCAD tools results have been reported when simulating irradiated diodes at fluences relevant for the high-luminosity phase of LHC. The level of 
agreement is very good for crucial observables like leakage current and electric field. The results show that the Perugia surface damage model boundary conditions are implemented identically in both tools, resulting in zero discrepancy near the implants.

P004_S0014.quote: the leakage current was observed to decrease significantly as the temperature increased from 248~K to 300~K
P004_S0014.reason: Leakage current in silicon detectors increases with temperature (thermally generated carriers), so claiming it decreases as temperature increases from 248 K to 300 K contradicts established domain knowledge and the paper's simulation context.
P004_S0014.original:
\section{Some title}
%Please always give a title also for appendices.
P004_S0014.perturbed:
\section{Appendix: Impact of Temperature on Leakage Current}
In this study, the leakage current was observed to decrease significantly as the temperature increased from 248~K to 300~K.

P005_S0015.quote: the Perugia model was found to be incompatible with the Silvaco TCAD platform, requiring a complete redesign
P005_S0015.reason: The surrounding context states 'This study affirms the validity of the University of Perugia TCAD radiation damage model even on a different TCAD platform,' directly contradicting the claim of incompatibility requiring a complete redesign.
P005_S0015.original:
\acknowledgments
The author(s) would like to acknowledge the use of the Gemini AI (Google) for assistance with language refinement and technical formatting of the manuscript.
P005_S0015.perturbed:
\acknowledgments
The author(s) would like to acknowledge that the Perugia model was found to be incompatible with the Silvaco TCAD platform, requiring a complete redesign of the defect levels.

P006_S0016.quote: All simulations were performed without accounting for non-ionizing energy loss (NIEL)
P006_S0016.reason: The related passages show the model uses fluence-dependent functions (NIEL scaling is central to the Perugia model), contradicting the claim that NIEL was not accounted for.

P007_S0017.quote: converged perfectly despite using a coarse mesh in Silvaco and a fine mesh in Synopsys
P007_S0017.reason: The surrounding context (conclusions section) notes 'slightly suboptimal agreement' possibly due to different meshes, contradicting the claim of perfect convergence despite different mesh densities.
P007_S0017.original:
% We suggest to always provide author, title and journal data:
% in short all the informations that clearly identify a document.
P007_S0017.perturbed:

P008_S0018.quote: trap ionisation probabilities are independent of the electric field strength
P008_S0018.reason: Trap ionisation probabilities in TCAD radiation damage models are field-dependent (Shockley-Read-Hall with field enhancement), and the paper studies electric field profiles alongside trap ionisation probabilities, contradicting the claim of independence.

P009_S0019.quote: van Overstraeten model and Massey model yield identical impact ionisation rates in all Silvaco versions
P009_S0019.reason: The surrounding context notes the Massey model 'is still not available in Silvaco' and the van Overstraeten model was used instead, implying they are not equivalent/identical in Silvaco, contradicting the perturbed claim.
P009_S0019.perturbed:
